summary: Stellar Let It Grow and epic Uncle John's > Playing > Terrapin suite
blurb: The show kicks off with a lively Iko Iko and builds to a soaring Let It Grow. The second set delivers an over‑the‑top Uncle John’s Band → Playing In The Band → Terrapin Station suite, capped by a powerful Terrapin Station jam and a heartfelt Wharf Rat. Highlights also include Brent’s organ work on New Minglewood Blues and the closing Black Muddy River.
review: Alpine Valley on June 27, 1987 opened with an exuberant Iko Iko that set a bright, bouncing mood for the evening. Jerry’s rhythm chops shone on New Minglewood Blues while his lead on Let It Grow was described as “soaring,” and even when he slipped a lyric on Wharf Rat the band kept the energy high – Phil’s crowd‑chant and quick‑witted banter only added to the fun. The second set is where this night truly shines: an over‑the‑top Uncle John’s Band → Playing In The Band → Terrapin Station sequence that fans call legendary, flowing seamlessly into a thunderous Terrapin Station > Drums > Space jam before launching into Truckin’, The Other One and a soulful Wharf Rat. Brent’s Hammond organ added color on Minglewood and his “fiddlish synth” solo on Friend of the Devil was a standout moment. The night closed with an uplifting Black Muddy River, leaving the audience buzzing under the summer sky.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five recordings are clearly positive, praising the Let It Grow performance, the Uncle John’s > Playing > Terrapin suite and overall energy; one mixed review notes solid playing despite minor speed issues, while a single negative review is an outlier. The strong consensus of praise yields a moderate‑high sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Most complete audience tape with decent balance; captures the standout Let It Grow and Terrapin moments despite fair clarity, making it the go‑to reference for this show.

key_highlights: Soaring Let It Grow early in Set 1
Over‑the‑top Uncle John’s Band → Playing In The Band → Terrapin Station suite
Powerful Terrapin Station > Drums > Space jam leading into Truckin’/The Other One and Wharf Rat
Energetic Iko Iko opener and closing Black Muddy River

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a strong rhythm foundation on New Minglewood Blues, leads an inspiring Let It Grow, and despite a lyric slip on Wharf Rat keeps the vibe high.
band_performance.Phil: Gets a crowd chant of “We want Phil,” adds witty banter during Tom Thumb Blues, and helps cue Jerry’s lyric reminder on Wharf Rat.
band_performance.Brent: Hammond organ shines on New Minglewood Blues; his ‘fiddlish synth’ solo on Friend Of The Devil is highlighted as a high point of the night.
